rows.notes: Clear, centered year at top of page, no monetary columns present.
Large, well-written page title. No amounts in same line.
Date line directly under title; clearly legible and contains no monetary figures.
Section heading for receipts. No numeric amounts on the same text line.
Pounds '743', shillings '17', pence shows '0' with a small quarter-mark (rendered as '1/4') — alignment with vertical ruling lines is clear and digits are distinct.

The figures appear as '8 10 4' to the right; however the hundreds-digit area is faint and could conceivably be '8' (small sum) or '8xx' if ink truncated. Chose 8 pounds, 10s, 4d based on spacing between the vertical column lines and the visible '8' in the pounds column.

Pounds read as '497' (the first digit of the hundreds is somewhat compressed but legible), shillings '19' clear, pence '6' with a halfpenny sign (interpreted as 0.5). There is some ink faintness around the hundreds digit so moderate confidence.
Underlined subtotal number is clear: '2750' pounds, '6' shillings, '10' pence with '3/4' (three-quarters) mark. Vertical alignment with ruling lines is unambiguous.
Clear reading: '1460' pounds, '3' shillings, '6' pence with a halfpenny sign (0.5). Column boundaries align well with other rows.
Underlined grand total for the receipts block is clear: '4210' pounds, '10' shillings, '5' pence with a quarter-mark (1/4). Good alignment with ruling lines.
Section heading for payments/expenditures. No monetary figures on the same line.
Clear reading: pounds '711', shillings '1', pence '0' with a small quarter mark (1/4). Text abbreviation is faint but legible; numeric columns align well.
Pounds '399' and shillings '10' are legible. Pence '2' shows a halfpenny sign (0.5). Minor flourish on the final digit introduced slight ambiguity but digits fall clearly within the pence column.
Pounds read as '1659' though the '1' at left is somewhat faint; shillings '2' and pence '2' with a halfpenny mark (0.5) visible. Alignment with neighboring numeric lines supports this reading.
Underlined subtotal for payments clearly reads '2769' pounds, '13' shillings, '5' pence with quarter mark (1/4). Well aligned and distinct.
'Ex Receptis' is followed by the receipts total repeated: '4210 10 5 1/4' — clear and matches earlier total. Labeled row (treated as total).
Clear labeled deduction line: '2769 13 5 1/4' matching the payments subtotal. Numbers are underlined and well aligned.
'Restat' (balance) reads '1440 17 0' clearly; no fraction indicated. Underline beneath value is visible and digits align correctly.
Certification heading preceding signatures. Text is legible and contains no numbers.
First signature-line. Handwriting is legible as a name and office; some flourish but no monetary amounts. Slight uncertainty on exact punctuation and title abbreviation.

Second signature appears to read 'J. L. Richards Subrectorem' — 'Subrectorem' is an inferred Latin office abbreviation; handwriting is cursive and a little compressed causing moderate uncertainty in exact spelling of surname.

Third signature uncertain: letters look like 'C. W. Tuffor' followed by an abbreviation that may be 'Bursar' and 'Senr.' The surname and abbreviations are not fully clear due to flourishes—moderate to low confidence.
Fourth signature likely 'J. P. Lyttfort Decanum' (or similar). The surname and office are legible but have unusual letter-forms; moderate confidence.
Fifth signature appears to be 'H. D. Harrington' — handwriting slightly faded but readable; no amounts present.
Final signature line reads 'W. Falconer' (or similar); letters are fairly clear though there is minor ink bleed at the end of the name.
